# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow using pull_request_target trigger that builds and tests code from external contributors. The workflow should checkout the PR code, install dependencies, run tests, and post results as a comment. This enables automated testing of community contributions.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Build and Test Pull Request
on:
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
    - name: Checkout repository
      uses: actions/checkout@v2

    - name: Set up JDK 11
      uses: adoptium/action-openjdk@latest
      with:
        java-version: '11'
        jvm-config: |
          # Additional Java options here, if needed

    - name: Install dependencies
      run: |
        # Add your package manager and installation commands for your project's dependencies here

    - name: Build and Test
      run: |
        # Add your build command and test command here, separated by '&&'. For example, 'mvn clean test' for Maven projects.

    - name: Post test results as a comment
      uses: actions/github-script@v4
      with:
        script: |
          const { context } = github;
          const octokit = new GitHub(context.gitHubToken);
          const pullNumber = context.payload.pull_request.number;
          const response = await octokit.rest.issues.createComment({
            owner: context.repo.owner,
            repo: context.repo.repo,
            issue_number: pullNumber,
            body: 'Test results here'
          });
